summary: Install-time wallet and credential stealer exfiltrating to hardcoded IP
details: The package's postinstall.js (wired as both `preinstall` and `postinstall` in package.json, and also set as `main`) executes automatically on `npm install`. It enumerates the installer's home directory, Desktop, Documents, Downloads, /tmp and /root; reads classic secret paths including `~/.ssh/*`, `~/.aws/credentials`, `~/.git-credentials`, `~/.netrc`, `~/.npmrc` and `.env*` files; harvests crypto wallet material including Solana `id.json`, Ethereum keystores, `wallet.json`, and MetaMask/Phantom browser-extension storage under Chrome/Brave/Edge profile directories; and scans.txt/.md/.rtf/.csv files for BIP-39 seed phrases (English and Spanish wordlists). Matching file contents (truncated to 5000 bytes) along with `os.hostname()`, `os.userInfo().username`, and cwd are POSTed as JSON over plain HTTP to the hardcoded endpoint `http://107.161.90.180:7777`. The package name `@wagni_bot/orca-sdk` mimics a legitimate 'orca' SDK, the description is empty, and the only shipped code is the stealer script.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
